title: memstead-plugin/v0 ingest
description: An ingest config wires a projection to an execution mode and trigger. Multiple ingests may target the same projection (e.g. one for manual discovery, another for continuous refinement). Each file lives at `.memstead/ingests/<name>.json`. The `projection` reference uses the form `<vault>/<name>` (no `.json` suffix), matching the on-disk path under `.memstead/projections/`.

mode.description: How the agent runs the projection. `discovery` enumerates sources for the first time; `refinement` improves an already-built graph; `one-shot` runs once and stops (typical for planning lenses).

trigger.description: When the ingest fires. `loop` runs continuously under `/loop`; `manual` runs only when invoked; `on-event` reserved for future event-driven hooks.

batch_size.description: Optional source-artifact batch size for refinement runs. Ignored by other modes.

deny_paths.description: Optional array of project-root-relative paths the ingest agent must not Read/Glob/Grep while this ingest runs. Resolved against the project root (nearest `.git/`), not against the workspace `.memstead.toml` directory. Each entry may name a file (e.g. `VISION.md`) or a directory (e.g. `dev`); a directory entry blocks the directory itself and everything under it, plus glob patterns whose literal prefix lies inside it. Absent or empty array means the hook permits every read — default-open. The portability rationale: the plugin makes no assumption about a workspace's framing layout; each ingest declares its own visibility policy, which becomes the documentation of what the running agent must not see.

post_actions.description: Optional post-run actions. Currently only `archive_source` is recognised (see plugins/claude-code/skills/ingest/README.md).

archive_source.description: When `true`, the inject prompt instructs the agent to archive the source vault after every destination is processed (used for one-shot lens ingests).
